msgSender().details: The other context functions, _msgData and _msgValue, are not supported by this contract In many contracts this will be the address that calls the initial entry point that calls `_executeActions` `msg.sender` shouldn't be used, as this will be the v4 pool manager contract that calls `unlockCallback` If using ReentrancyLock.sol, this function can return _getLocker()

unlockCallback(bytes).details: We force the onlyPoolManager modifier by exposing a virtual function after the onlyPoolManager check.

params.data: The data that was passed to the call to unlock

returns._0: Any data that you want to be returned from the unlock call

msgSender().notice: function that returns address considered executor of the actions

unlockCallback(bytes).notice: Called by the pool manager on `msg.sender` when the manager is unlocked
